Login Feature Requirements

This page defines the acceptance criteria and user journey for the Login feature of the ExampleApp platform.

Overview

Users must be able to authenticate using email/password credentials, social login providers (Google, GitHub), or SSO. The login page must be accessible, performant, and secure against common attack vectors.

Acceptance Criteria

  • AC-1: User can log in with a valid email and password and is redirected to the dashboard
  • AC-2: User sees an inline error message when entering an invalid email format
  • AC-3: User sees an error message after entering incorrect credentials
  • AC-4: User account is locked after 5 consecutive failed login attempts
  • AC-5: "Remember Me" checkbox persists the session for 30 days
  • AC-6: User can log in via Google OAuth 2.0
  • AC-7: User can log in via GitHub OAuth
  • AC-8: Session expires after 30 minutes of inactivity
  • AC-9: Login form is fully navigable via keyboard (Tab, Enter, Escape)
  • AC-10: Screen readers can identify all form fields and error messages via ARIA labels
  • AC-11: Concurrent sessions from different devices are allowed up to a maximum of 3
  • AC-12: Passwords with special characters (e.g., <, >, &, ") are handled correctly

User Journey

  1. User navigates to /login
  2. User enters email in the email input field
  3. User enters password in the password input field
  4. User optionally checks "Remember Me"
  5. User clicks the "Sign In" button
  6. System validates credentials against the authentication service
  7. On success: user is redirected to /dashboard with a welcome toast
  8. On failure: inline error is displayed below the form, input fields retain values

Edge Cases

  • User submits the form with both fields empty
  • User pastes a very long string (>256 chars) into the password field
  • User navigates away mid-login and returns
  • User opens login in multiple tabs simultaneously
  • Network timeout during credential validation

Security Considerations

  • Rate limiting: max 10 login attempts per IP per minute
  • CSRF token required on form submission
  • Password field must mask input and disable autocomplete
  • All login attempts are logged for audit purposes
